diagrams.title: Access Control Models Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the relationship and evolution from traditional to modern access control models, from most restrictive (MAC) to most flexible (ABAC). This helps students understand how access control has evolved and the trade-offs between control and flexibility.
elements: Labels for control level (High to Low)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the evolution and hierarchy of access control models from most restrictive to most flexible

diagrams.title: Access Control Decision Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the step-by-step process of how an access request moves through an access control system, from user request to final grant/deny decision. Includes key components like authentication, authorization, and policy evaluation.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the access control decision process and the components involved in granting or denying access

diagrams.title: Access Control Models Comparison
diagrams.description: A visual comparison chart showing the four main access control models side-by-side, highlighting their key characteristics including control level, management approach, flexibility, and common use cases.
elements: MAC row: Government/military, Label-based, High security
DAC row: Owner-controlled, Permission-based, File sharing
RBAC row: Organization-based, Role assignment, Enterprise systems
ABAC row: Policy-based, Dynamic attributes, Cloud environments
diagrams.purpose: Provides a quick reference for comparing different access control models and their appropriate use cases
